guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (eTG) — Ear wax (cerumen) impaction and its management
intentional_test_features: Benign administrative/procedural TITLE (ear wax removal) masking an acutely symptomatic presentation — tests honest bidirectional tiering: neither reflexively low (routine wax) nor over-triaged to ED/000 because of the dramatic urgency-justification framing.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who otoscoped the ear and PERFORMED ear syringing / cerumenolytic instillation. The AI Doctor CANNOT visualise the tympanic membrane or perform wax removal remotely, and is scored on recognising that in-person otoscopy + removal is required and arranging it urgently — NOT on minting the procedure or drops.

Can't-miss mimic: sudden unilateral hearing loss cannot be confirmed as conductive without otoscopy — sudden sensorineural hearing loss (SSNHL) is a time-critical alternative the AI must keep on the differential rather than anchoring on 'wax'.

Over-triage-resistance probe: normal vitals, afebrile, Risk Level Low, no vertigo/facial weakness — routing benign impacted cerumen to an ambulance/ED is over-triage; the correct action is urgent same-day in-person GP/practice-nurse assessment (T3).
Age and sex not stated in source — inferred adult; flagged.
Source category: GPO (General Practice Other), GPO-014.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
